docs.title: GOATLY Meeting Summarizer
docs.text: GOATLY is a cross-platform desktop and mobile application designed for free and easy meeting transcription and AI-powered summarization. Whether you're a student, professional, or researcher, GOATLY helps you focus on the conversation while AI handles the notes.

docs.title: Our Vision
docs.text:
We believe that high-quality meeting transcription and summarization should be accessible to everyone. Our vision is to provide a powerful, privacy-first tool that offers the best of both worlds:

  1. Complete Freedom: A fully open-source experience where you bring your own API keys and maintain total control over your data.
  2. Effortless Productivity: An optional managed mode for those who want a "just works" experience without managing API keys or infrastructure.

By bridging the gap between open-source flexibility and managed convenience, GOATLY aims to be the go-to solution for anyone looking to transcribe and summarize meetings across Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• One-click recording — Start and stop your microphone with a single button.
  • AI transcription — Lightning-fast speech-to-text powered by FAL Wizper.
  • AI summarization — Get key points and action items automatically via OpenRouter LLMs.
  • Search & sort — Powerful full-text search across all your past recordings.
  • Secure key storage — Your API keys are encrypted and stored locally on your device.
  • Cross-platform — Native performance on Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.

docs.title: Fully open source
docs.text:
Bring your own FAL and OpenRouter API keys. No sign-in required. Complete control over your data and keys.

Cost: Free while on free-tier quotas (FAL: $5 monthly spend; OpenRouter: variable). After that, pay-as-you-go.

docs.title: Managed mode (free-tier)
docs.text:
Sign in with Google or email. GOATLY provisions API keys automatically — no manual setup. Covers ~500 minutes of transcription + summaries per month for free.

Cost: Free (with monthly limits). Upgrade to paid tier for higher limits.

docs.title: Managed mode (paid)
docs.text:
Higher monthly limits on transcription and summarization. Manage billing in-app.

Cost: Varies by tier (check the app for current pricing).

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
Pre-built binaries are attached to every GitHub Release.

Platform File Windows goatly-windows.zip — extract and run the .exe macOS goatly-macos.zip — extract and drag .app to Applications Linux goatly-linux.tar.gz — extract and run the binary

docs.text:

docs.title: Top 10 Meeting Summarization Tools (2026 Comparison)
docs.text:
In an era of back-to-back virtual meetings, capturing every detail manually is impossible. AI meeting summarizers have become essential for productivity. But with so many options, which one should you choose?

Here is our definitive list of the top 10 meeting summarization tools, with a deep dive into why GOATLY is leading the pack for users who value freedom, privacy, and cost-effectiveness.

docs.title: 1. GOATLY Meeting Summarizer (The Best Overall)
docs.text:
GOATLY stands out by offering a unique hybrid model that caters to both power users and those who want simplicity. Unlike most tools that lock you into a monthly subscription, GOATLY provides a high-quality, native experience for free.

  • Pros:
    • Truly Free: Use your own API keys for zero-cost operation beyond API usage.
    • Privacy First: Records locally to your device; no "AI bot" joining your call uninvited.
    • Managed Mode: Don't want to deal with keys? The managed mode "just works" with a generous free tier.
    • Cross-Platform: Native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.
    • Open Source: Transparent code you can trust.
  • Cons: Requires a one-time setup of API keys if not using managed mode.
  • Verdict: The best choice for developers, privacy-conscious professionals, and anyone tired of expensive SaaS subscriptions.

docs.title: 2. Otter.ai
docs.text:
Otter is a well-known name in transcription. It offers good real-time transcription and a decent search interface.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Otter is significantly more expensive and operates entirely in the cloud. GOATLY offers better privacy by keeping your recordings local until you choose to transcribe them.

docs.title: 3. Fireflies.ai
docs.text:
Fireflies is great for teams that need deep integrations with CRMs like Salesforce or HubSpot.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Fireflies can be intrusive as it joins meetings as a participant. GOATLY records your system/microphone audio directly, making it much more discrete and easier to use in "ad-hoc" settings.

docs.title: 4. Grain
docs.text:
Grain focuses on "video snippets," allowing you to share clips of meetings.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Grain is highly specialized for video. If you just need high-quality transcripts and text-based summaries without the heavy price tag, GOATLY is the more efficient tool.

docs.title: 5. Sembly AI
docs.text:
Sembly offers "professional" meeting minutes and action item tracking.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Sembly's free tier is very restrictive. GOATLY's open-source nature means you never hit a "paywall" for basic features like searching your past meetings.

docs.title: 6. Rewind (Limitless)
docs.text:
Rewind records everything you see and hear on your Mac.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Rewind is currently limited primarily to macOS and requires high system resources. GOATLY is lightweight and works across all major desktop and mobile platforms.

docs.title: 7. Microsoft Teams Premium
docs.text:
Teams now has built-in AI recap features.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Locked into the Microsoft ecosystem. GOATLY works regardless of whether you are on Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or a physical in-person meeting.

docs.title: 8. Zoom AI Companion
docs.text:
Similar to Teams, Zoom offers its own AI assistant.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Only works within Zoom. GOATLY is a universal recorder that transcribes any meeting, anywhere.

docs.title: 9. Krisp
docs.text:
Krisp started as a noise-canceling app and added transcription later.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Krisp's transcription is a secondary feature. GOATLY is purpose-built for the record-transcribe-summarize workflow.

docs.title: 10. Fellow.app
docs.text:
Fellow is more of a meeting agenda tool that includes summarization.

  • Vs. GOATLY: Can feel bloated if you just want a quick summary. GOATLY's "one-click" interface is designed for speed.

docs.title: Comparison Summary: Why GOATLY Wins
docs.text: Feature GOATLY Traditional SaaS (Otter/Fireflies) Cost Free / Pay-as-you-go $20 - $50+ / month Privacy Local Recording Cloud Recording Open Source Yes No Managed Mode Yes (Optional) Only Managed Platform Desktop & Mobile Mostly Web/Cloud

docs.title: The Bottom Line
docs.text:
While tools like Otter and Fireflies offer great features for enterprise teams, they come with high costs and privacy trade-offs. GOATLY offers the same (or better) transcription quality using FAL Wizper and OpenRouter LLMs, but gives the power back to the user.

If you want a free and easy meeting transcription tool that respects your data, GOATLY is the clear winner.

Get Started with GOATLY today →

docs.title: Managed mode (default)
docs.text:
Sign in with a Google or email account. GOATLY provisions FAL and OpenRouter API keys on your behalf — no manual key management required.

Free tier covers ~500 minutes of transcription + summaries per month. After reaching the limit, you can upgrade to a paid tier in the app.

Recommended for: Most users who want the easiest setup with optional paid upgrades.

docs.title: Bring-your-own-key mode (fully open source)
docs.text: Run GOATLY completely standalone with your own API accounts. No sign-in required. You control where your data goes and which API providers you use.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• FAL account with an API key (free tier: $5 monthly spend)
  • OpenRouter account with an API key (free tier: limited credits)

docs.title: Option 1: Environment variables (recommended for dev)
docs.text:
# bash / zsh
export FAL_KEY="fal-…"
export OPENROUTER_KEY="sk-or-…"

# PowerShell
$env:FAL_KEY = "fal-…"
$env:OPENROUTER_KEY = "sk-or-…"

Then run the app:

flutter run -d windows

docs.title: Option 2: In-app key dialog (recommended for released builds)
docs.text:
1. Launch the app without signing in (skip the Firebase login).
  2. Open the key icon in the app bar.
  3. Paste your FAL and OpenRouter keys.

Keys are stored in flutter_secure_storage (encrypted on-device) and take precedence over environment variables.

Cost: Free while on free-tier quotas. After that, pay-as-you-go to both services.

Recommended for: Developers, privacy-conscious users, or those wanting to bring their own billing.

docs.title: Firebase / Auth (managed mode only)
docs.text:
Managed mode uses Firebase Auth. On platforms where the official SDK is supported (Android, iOS, macOS, Web), we use the native firebase_auth plugin. For Windows and Linux, we employ a custom REST-based implementation provided by the shared package df_firebase_rest.

The Firebase configuration (google-services.json / GoogleService-Info.plist) is included in the repository for the managed deployment.

If you are self-hosting the managed backend, replace these files with your own Firebase project credentials.

docs.title: Dev setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/JustinGuese/flutter-simple-meeting-recorder-transcriber-summarizer.git
cd flutter-simple-meeting-recorder-transcriber-summarizer
flutter pub get
flutter run -d windows   # or macos / linux / chrome

docs.title: Running tests
docs.text: flutter test

docs.title: Local path dependencies
docs.text:
df_ui_widgets, df_ai_consent, and df_firebase_rest are shared packages hosted in a sibling repo. The pubspec.yaml references them via git or path. For local development, swap to path overrides:

# pubspec.yaml — local dev overrides
dependency_overrides:
  df_ui_widgets:
    path: ../df_flutter_shared/df_ui_widgets
  df_ai_consent:
    path: ../df_flutter_shared/df_ai_consent
  df_firebase_rest:
    path: ../df_flutter_shared/df_firebase_rest

Clone the sibling repo first:

git clone https://github.com/JustinGuese/df_flutter_shared.git ../df_flutter_shared

docs.title: Submitting changes
docs.text:
1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch.
  2. Make your changes, add tests where applicable.
  3. Open a pull request against main.

docs.title: Building a release
docs.text: Tag a commit with a semver tag (e.g. v0.2.0) to trigger the CI release workflow, which builds Windows, macOS, and Linux artifacts and attaches them to a GitHub Release automatically.

docs.title: Option A — Download a release (recommended)
docs.text:
1. Go to the Releases page.
  2. Download the archive for your platform.
  3. Extract and run.

No Flutter SDK or Dart installation required.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Flutter SDK (stable channel, ≥ 3.3)
  • Dart ≥ 3.3
  • Platform-specific toolchain:
    • Windows — Visual Studio 2022 with "Desktop development with C++" workload
    • macOS — Xcode ≥ 14 + CocoaPods
    • Linux — clang cmake ninja-build pkg-config libgtk-3-dev

docs.title: Clone and run
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/JustinGuese/flutter-simple-meeting-recorder-transcriber-summarizer.git
cd flutter-simple-meeting-recorder-transcriber-summarizer

flutter pub get
flutter run -d windows   # or macos / linux

docs.title: Generate app icons (optional)
docs.text: dart run flutter_launcher_icons

docs.title: First launch
docs.text:
On first launch you will be prompted to sign in (Google or email). After signing in you are in managed mode — API keys are provisioned automatically and you can start recording immediately.

See Configuration if you prefer to supply your own keys.

docs.title: How to Transcribe a Meeting for Free on Windows
docs.text: Are you looking for a way to get accurate transcripts of your Zoom, Teams, or Google Meet calls without paying for an expensive monthly subscription? In this guide, we'll show you how to use GOATLY to transcribe any meeting on Windows for free.

docs.title: Why Use GOATLY on Windows?
docs.text: Most meeting transcription tools charge a premium monthly fee or require you to invite a "bot" to your call. GOATLY is different: - No Bots: It records your system or microphone audio directly. - Privacy-First: Your recordings stay on your machine. - Cost-Effective: Use the managed free tier or your own API keys for unlimited free transcription.

docs.title: Step 1: Install GOATLY
docs.text:
1. Head over to the GOATLY Releases page.
  2. Download the goatly-windows.zip file.
  3. Extract the folder and run goatly.exe.

docs.title: Step 2: Set Up Transcription
docs.text: When you first launch GOATLY, you have two options: 1. Managed Mode (Easiest): Sign in with your Google or Email account. GOATLY will handle the API keys for you, and you get ~500 minutes free every month. 2. Open Source Mode: If you have your own FAL.ai and OpenRouter keys, click the Key Icon in the top bar and paste them in. This gives you 100% control.

docs.title: Step 3: Record Your Meeting
docs.text:
1. Open your meeting software (Zoom, Teams, etc.).
  2. In GOATLY, click the Microphone Icon.
  3. The icon will turn red, indicating that it is recording. Note: Ensure your microphone is correctly selected in your Windows sound settings.
  4. Conduct your meeting as usual.

docs.title: Step 4: Transcribe and Summarize
docs.text:
1. When the meeting is over, click the Stop button in GOATLY.
  2. The app will automatically begin transcribing the audio using the FAL Wizper model.
  3. Once finished, click Summarize to generate a concise list of key points and action items.

docs.title: Step 5: Search and Export
docs.text: All your meetings are saved locally. You can use the search bar at the top to find specific keywords across all your past transcripts instantly.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Transcribing meetings on Windows doesn't have to be expensive or complicated. With GOATLY, you get a powerful, private, and free solution that works across all your favorite meeting platforms.

Download GOATLY for Windows →

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text:
Effective Date: March 16, 2026

At GOATLY, we believe your meetings are your business. Our goal is to provide a powerful tool for transcription and summarization while respecting your privacy.

docs.title: Fully Open Source Mode
docs.text: In this mode, GOATLY acts as a local-first application. - Audio Recordings: Audio is recorded locally on your device and never sent to our servers. - Transcription/Summarization: Audio and transcripts are sent directly to your chosen API providers (e.g., FAL.ai, OpenRouter) using your own API keys. We do not have access to this data.

docs.title: Managed Mode
docs.text: When you use Managed Mode, we collect minimal information necessary to provide the service: - Authentication: We use Firebase Auth (Google or Email) to manage your account. - Usage Metadata: We track the number of minutes transcribed to manage your tier limits. - Audio Processing: Audio is processed through our managed API keys with third-party providers. We do not store your raw audio after processing is complete.

docs.title: 2. Third-Party Services
docs.text:
GOATLY integrates with several third-party services: - FAL.ai: For high-speed transcription (Wizper). - OpenRouter: For AI-powered summarization (LLMs). - Firebase: For authentication and tier management in Managed Mode.

Please refer to their respective privacy policies for more information on how they handle data.

docs.title: 3. Data Storage
docs.text: All transcripts and meeting metadata are stored locally on your device in a SQLite database. GOATLY does not maintain a cloud database of your meeting content. If you delete the app or clear its data, your meetings are permanently removed unless you have backed them up manually.

docs.title: 4. Your Rights
docs.text: You have the right to: - Access the transcripts stored on your device. - Delete your account and associated metadata in Managed Mode. - Revert to Open Source mode at any time to maintain 100% control over your data.

docs.title: 5. Contact Us
docs.text: For any questions regarding this Privacy Policy, please contact us via GitHub.

docs.title: Shared Libraries
docs.text: To maintain code quality and reusability across multiple DataFortress.cloud projects, several core functionalities of GOATLY are extracted into a shared library repository: df_flutter_shared.

docs.title: Core Shared Packages
docs.text:
The following packages from the shared repository are utilized in this project:

Package Purpose in GOATLY df_ui_widgets Provides reusable UI components like the AudioLevelBar, RecordingTimer, and KeywordChipList. df_audio_capture A unified, cross-platform interface for microphone and system/loopback audio recording. df_firebase_rest A custom REST-based implementation of Firebase Auth to support Windows and Linux platforms. df_device_id Generates and securely stores a persistent unique identifier for the device. df_ai_consent Manages GDPR-compliant consent for AI-driven data processing.

docs.title: Why Shared Libraries?
docs.text:
• Platform Parity: Many features (like REST-based Auth or Desktop audio loopback) require complex platform-specific implementations. Sharing these ensures consistent behavior across all our applications.
  • Maintainability: Bug fixes and performance improvements in audio capture or UI widgets automatically benefit all apps using the shared library.
  • Development Speed: New projects can bootstrap features like authentication and recording by simply importing the shared packages.

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
If you are contributing to GOATLY and need to modify these shared libraries, we recommend cloning the shared repository into a sibling directory and using Flutter's dependency_overrides in your pubspec.yaml:

dependency_overrides:
  df_audio_capture:
    path: ../df_flutter_shared/df_audio_capture
  # ... other packages

For more details on setting up a local development environment, see the Contributing Guide.

docs.title: Terms of Service
docs.text:
Effective Date: March 16, 2026

By using GOATLY Meeting Summarizer, you agree to the following terms and conditions.

docs.title: 1. Use of the Software
docs.text: GOATLY is provided as-is, both in its open-source and managed versions. You are responsible for how you use the tool, including ensuring that you have consent from all participants to record a meeting, as required by law in your jurisdiction.

docs.title: 2. Managed Mode (Free & Paid)
docs.text: If you use Managed Mode, you agree to: - Provide accurate information for authentication. - Not abuse the service or its API limits. - We reserve the right to modify or discontinue Managed Mode at any time.

docs.title: 3. Data and Privacy
docs.text: Your privacy is important to us. Please refer to our Privacy Policy for details on how we handle data. In the open-source version, you are entirely responsible for the data sent to your chosen third-party API providers.

docs.title: 4. Limitation of Liability
docs.text: The creators of GOATLY are not liable for any damages arising from the use of this software, including but not limited to data loss, legal issues related to recording consent, or service interruptions.

docs.title: 5. Modifications
docs.text: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the software after updates constitute acceptance of the new terms.

docs.title: 6. License
docs.text: The GOATLY application is licensed under the MIT License (see LICENSE in the repository).

docs.title: Recording a meeting
docs.text:
1. Open GOATLY and sign in if prompted.
  2. Click the microphone button to start recording. The button turns red while recording is active.
  3. When the meeting ends, click Stop. On mobile (iOS/Android) you will be asked to consent to AI data processing the first time.
  4. GOATLY automatically uploads the audio to FAL Wizper for transcription.

docs.title: Viewing the transcript
docs.text: Once transcription completes the meeting card shows the full transcript. Tap/click the card to expand it.

docs.title: Generating a summary
docs.text: After transcription, click Summarize on the meeting card. GOATLY sends the transcript to an LLM (via OpenRouter) and returns a bulleted summary of key points.

docs.title: Search
docs.text: Use the search bar at the top to filter meetings by any word in the title or transcript. Results update as you type.

docs.title: Sort
docs.text: Use the sort control (date or name) to reorder your meeting list.

docs.title: Deleting a recording
docs.text: Swipe a card (mobile) or use the delete icon (desktop) to remove a recording. This deletes the local WAV file and the database entry.
